description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust metallic frame. The solar panels are dark, likely monocrystalline or polycrystalline silicon, and are arranged in a single row, slightly elevated above a base platform. The base appears to be constructed from metal, possibly steel or aluminum, and includes a grated or vented surface, suggesting provisions for airflow or cooling. The structure is low-profile and designed for ground installation, with a small protruding section that may house electrical components or serve as a mounting anchor. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for efficient solar energy capture in urban environments such as sidewalks, parks, or rooftops.
description: A compact, ground-mounted solar panel array with a metallic base and a single row of dark photovoltaic panels, designed for urban energy capture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rectangular solar panel array elevated above a grated metallic base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the solar panels and the extended base platform.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the panel array and the structure of the supporting frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the length of the base and the alignment of the solar panels.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metallic (likely aluminum or steel) frame and grated base
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 4 panels
